name: Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop
name_original: Vía Verde de los Montes del Hierro – circular desde Musques
description: Cycle the Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop, a difficult 25.3-mile route through Bizkaia's mining history and diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Muskiz traverse a diverse landscape in Bizkaia, Basque Country, characterized by its extensive coastline, dramatic cliffs, and the valley of the Barbadún River. The region features green mountains, including Mont Mello at 626 meters, and the ecologically valuable Pobeña Estuary. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from coastal paths to the continuous steep slopes of the low mountain range, providing a challenging yet rewarding experience. Best touring cycling routes around Muskiz The most popular touring cycling route is Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop, a 25.3 miles (40.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 17 minutes to complete. This route allows cyclists to explore the region's mining history. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valverde Tunnel – La Arena Beach loop from Muskiz, a difficult 46.4 miles (74.7 km) path. This route connects coastal areas with inland features, offering varied scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the La Arena Beach – Valverde Tunnel loop from Muskiz, a 37.9 miles (61.1 km) trail leading through coastal areas and historical sites, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around Muskiz is defined by its coastline, green mountains, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Muskiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 2300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Muskiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Muskiz?
A: Muskiz offers a diverse selection of over 15 touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Muskiz?
A: Muskiz's touring cycling routes feature varied terrain, from coastal paths with breathtaking sea views to the continuous steep slopes characteristic of the Basque Country's low mountain range. You'll encounter both rough sections and dedicated greenways, providing a good challenge without being overly extreme.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Muskiz?
A: Yes, Muskiz has several routes suitable for beginners or families. While many routes offer a challenge, there are at least 3 easy touring cycling routes. For a gentler experience, consider sections of the Itsaslur Greenway, which offers panoramic coastal views on a dedicated path.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes in Muskiz?
A: Muskiz's routes showcase a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle past the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/577101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Arena Beach&lt;/a&gt;, dramatic cliffs, and the ecologically valuable Pobeña Estuary. Historical landmarks include the 16th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38285843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pobal Ferrería (Ironworks)&lt;/a&gt; and the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1051258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muñatones Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/334578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Montes del Hierro&lt;/a&gt; also offers insights into the region's mining history.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Muskiz?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Muskiz are designed as loops. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085248267/montes-de-hierro-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 40.8 km long, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20511127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valverde Tunnel – La Arena Beach loop from Muskiz&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 74.7 km.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Muskiz?
A: The Basque Country, including Muskiz, is known for its green landscapes, suggesting a temperate climate. While specific seasonal data isn't provided, generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the potential heat of summer and the colder, wetter conditions of winter. These seasons also highlight the region's verdant scenery.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes in Muskiz?
A: Muskiz offers several difficult routes for experienced touring cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20511127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valverde Tunnel – La Arena Beach loop from Muskiz&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 74.7 km route with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40341918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Arena Beach – Valverde Tunnel loop from Muskiz&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 61.1 km and also features substantial climbs.

Q: Can I reach the touring cycling routes in Muskiz using public transport?
A: Muskiz is generally accessible, and while specific public transport links to every trailhead are not detailed, the region is well-connected. For routes originating in the center of Muskiz, local public transport options are likely available. For longer routes like the Muskiz to Bilbao Cycle Path, public transport can facilitate reaching starting points or returning from destinations like Bilbao.

Q: Are there any specific routes that highlight the industrial heritage of Muskiz?
A: Yes, the region's rich mining and industrial history is a key feature. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085248267/montes-de-hierro-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, allowing cyclists to explore this heritage. Additionally, the route to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38285843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pobal Ferrería (Ironworks)&lt;/a&gt; offers a direct connection to a 16th-century industrial site.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Muskiz?
A: The touring cycling routes in Muskiz are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an impressive average rating of 4.3 stars from over 180 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse scenery, which combines coastal views with green mountains and river valleys, as well as the challenging yet rewarding terrain.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes that pass through or start in Muskiz?
A: Yes, Muskiz serves as a starting point or passes through longer routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38196978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valverde Tunnel – Guggenheim Museum loop from La Sequilla&lt;/a&gt; is a significant 136 km route that connects the area to Bilbao and its famous museum, offering a comprehensive touring experience.
tips: This cycle path is an exemplary example of what a successful cycle infrastructure looks like. It takes you from the beach town of La Arena to the center of Barakaldo (or the other way around). The path is not a bicycle path that has been removed from the car path with street paint, but rather it is an independent path with a surface that is easy to roll on. At the beginning (or end) of the path you drive through a quiet valley on a slight incline.

Interesting museum dedicated to the history and technology of the steel industry in the Basque Country. Located in the town of Muskiz, the museum is housed in an old 18th century ironworks, restored and adapted for use as a museum. The museum has a collection of machinery and tools from the period, which show the evolution of steelmaking technology in the area. It also offers guided tours and workshops to learn in detail the history and technique of this ancient trade. A fascinating place to discover the industrial history of the region.
Much, much peace on these roads

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied surface, including both tarmac roads and dirt tracks. You'll encounter sections of asphalt and compacted earth. Due to the presence of unpaved segments, a gravel bike or mountain bike is recommended for touring cyclists. There are also seven tunnels and several walkways to navigate.

faqs.question: How difficult is this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Montes de Hierro Greenway is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty for cyclists with a reasonable level of fitness. While some sources describe it as an 'easy one-day route for initiated cyclists,' be aware that some segments may involve steep slopes and require good to very good fitness. It can be completed in a single day, but for a more leisurely pace, it can be divided into two stages.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in both natural beauty and historical significance. You'll cycle along the Greenway of the Montes del Hierro, passing structures from the area's iron mining past. A notable historical stop is the El Pobal Ironworks Museum. You'll also find scenic spots like the Beautiful view towards the village of Galdames, and eventually reach views of Playa de la Arena beach and the Bay of Biscay.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: The best season for this adventure is from late spring to early autumn. During these months, you can enjoy pleasant weather, blooming vegetation in spring, and warm summer days, making for an ideal cycling experience.

faqs.question: Is the Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, specific rules regarding dogs (leash requirements, waste disposal) can vary. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in more populated areas or near wildlife, and to carry waste bags. Check local regulations for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this greenway?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and cycle the Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop. It is a public greenway, freely accessible to cyclists and hikers.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail typically runs from Traslaviña station to the area around Muskiz or Kobaron. You can find parking facilities near these starting points. For example, parking is usually available in towns like Muskiz, which offers convenient access to the greenway.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach the trailheads?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is well-connected by public transport. You can conveniently access the trailheads via train, particularly from Bilbao. This makes it easy to plan your trip, whether you intend to complete the full loop or just a section, with options to return to Bilbao by train from various points.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. The majority of the loop follows the Vía Verde de los Montes de Hierro, but it also passes through parts of the Camino Natural Via Verde Montes de Hierro (tramo de Atxuriaga a Gallarta), Ezkerraldea -Meatzalde - Enkarterri, and the Camino Natural Via Verde de Galdames a Sopuerta.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse a spectacular mountainous region with dense green forests. The scenery is a unique blend of natural beauty and historical industrial heritage, featuring old loading bays, railway stations, bridges, and tunnels from the iron mining era. A significant highlight is the transition from the mountains to the scenic Basque coast, offering views of Playa de la Arena beach and cliffs overlooking the Bay of Biscay.

faqs.question: Is the Montes de Hierro Greenway Loop suitable for families?
faqs.answer: While much of the route is suitable for all skill levels, especially the paved sections, some segments may involve steep slopes and unpaved terrain. For families with younger children or those seeking a very easy ride, consider tackling shorter, flatter sections or splitting the route into two stages. A gravel bike or mountain bike is recommended for comfort on varied surfaces.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the greenway connects several towns and villages, you can find amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options along or near the route. Towns like Muskiz and others in the region offer places to refuel and rest.
